title: Lenovo taps Becky G for FIFA World Cup localized campaign | Campaign US
text:
Lenovo taps Becky G for FIFA World Cup localized campaign
News
The ‘Shower’ singer will help local artists design kits for designated soccer clubs using Lenovo’s AI-powered tools.
by
Julia Walker
April 30 2026
(Photo credit: Lenovo, used with permission)
Lenovo, the
official technology partner of the FIFA World Cup
, announced a program with singer Becky G for community artists to design kits for local soccer clubs.
Lenovo’s Your Club Your Canvas campaign will appoint designers, artists and filmmakers as creative directors for soccer clubs in the U.S., Brazil, Italy and China.
As the program’s global ambassador, Becky G will collaborate with each club’s creative director to design a limited football/soccer kit with Lenovo’s AI-powered tools.
The initiative, part of Lenovo’s Work for Humankind platform, will donate all kit sales proceeds to the participating clubs, according to Lenovo’s announcement.
The participating clubs include U.S. clubs Ballard FC and Salmon Bay FC, Italy’s Treviso FBC 1993, China’s Regimen United F.C. and Brazil’s Grêmio Osasco Audax Esporte Clube.
Along with being the official technology partner for FIFA, Lenovo is also the official technology partner for Formula 1. The company is partnering with both the men’s 2026 World Cup and the women’s 2027 World Cup.
In September, Lenovo hired LinkedIn, Google and Edelman vet
Santi Pochat as its VP of AI innovation
.
Lenovo was not immediately available to respond to Campaign’s requests for comment.
The company reported a 20% increase in year-over-year revenue to $18.8 billion at the start of its 2025 fiscal year — marking the tech giant’s third consecutive quarter of double-digit growth — and claimed its strong performance was “driven by AI” in its
earnings report
.
This story first appeared on
PRWeek U.S.
